title: CustomEvent Object
description: A CustomEvent is primarily used to send an event from a worker thread you've created back to your add-in, which is running in the primary thread. It's also possible for add-ins to cooperate and another add-in can trigger this event in your add-in by knowing the custom event id.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Add a handler to be notified when the event occurs.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Removes a handler from the event.

properties.name: eventId
properties.description: Returns the id that was assigned to this event when it was registered. Each custom event has it's own unique id.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the event - e.g. "DocumentOpening"

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: sender
properties.description: The object that is firing the event. For example, in the case of a command input event this will return the command.

add.title: CustomEvent.add Method
add.description: Add a handler to be notified when the event occurs.
add.return_description: Returns true if the addition of the handler was successful.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: The handler object to be called when this event is fired.

related_samples.name: Custom Event for Command Dialog
related_samples.description: Demonstrates using a custom event to process getting information in the background to display in a command dialog. This is an add-in and should be copied and pasted into an add-in project.

related_samples.name: Custom Event Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the ability to call into the main thread from a worker thread. This sample is an add-in. To use it, use the Scripts and Add-Ins command to create a new add-in. Delete all of the code in the newly created add-in and replace it with the code below. Have a model open that has a parameter named "Length". Load the add-in. The add-in will change the value of the parameter every two seconds using a random value between 1 and 10.

classType.title: CustomEvent.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

remove.title: CustomEvent.remove Method
remove.description: Removes a handler from the event.
remove.return_description: Returns true if removal of the handler was successful.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: The handler object to be removed from the event.

eventId.title: CustomEvent.eventId Property
eventId.description: Returns the id that was assigned to this event when it was registered. Each custom event has it's own unique id.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = customEvent_var.eventId

name.description: The name of the event - e.g. "DocumentOpening"

syntax.python:

objectType.title: CustomEvent.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = customEvent_var.objectType

sender.title: CustomEvent.sender Property
sender.description: The object that is firing the event. For example, in the case of a command input event this will return the command.
